error.signature: AI tells a tourist arriving in Thailand mid-April to plan business meetings or expect normal traffic during Songkran
error.regex: (thailand.*songkran|สงกรานต์|thai new year.*water|chiang mai.*water festival|bangkok.*songkran|thailand april 13|thai water festival)

verdict.summary: Songkran (สงกรานต์, Thai New Year) runs April 13-15 (national holiday, sometimes extended through April 17). Cities erupt in nationwide water-throwing — buckets, water pistols, hoses, ice. Foreigners are prime targets. Government / banks / many businesses closed. Roads jammed (民族 大移動 equivalent). Traditional element: gentle water sprinkle on Buddha statues + elders' hands; tourist version is full soak. Phones / cameras / electronics need waterproof bags.

dead_ends.action: Schedule Bangkok / Chiang Mai business meetings April 13-17
dead_ends.why_fails: National holiday + water festival = no business. Government, banks, many corporates closed. Even basic services (post, courier) reduced. Traffic to/from airports doubled by holidaymakers. Reschedule for April 7-12 or April 20+.

dead_ends.action: Walk through central Chiang Mai or Khao San Bangkok in normal clothes during Songkran with phone in hand
dead_ends.why_fails: You will be soaked. Repeatedly. By multiple people. Phone, camera, wallet at risk. Locals + tourists shoot water at every passerby — refusing or showing irritation is bad form. Either join with water gun + waterproof bag, or stay indoors / avoid Old City / Khao San.

dead_ends.action: Drive a motorbike through Songkran water-throwing zone
dead_ends.why_fails: Water-soaked riders, slippery streets, sudden buckets to face — Songkran has the highest road-fatality week in Thailand (~400+ deaths annually nationwide, 'seven dangerous days'). Police checkpoints + alcohol sales restricted at times. Take taxi / Grab / songthaew; not motorbike.

workarounds.action: Plan Thailand trips around Songkran: arrive April 16+ (catch tail end / quiet) or before April 13. Or fully embrace it: stay near festival areas (Chiang Mai Old City moat, Bangkok Khao San), buy water gun + waterproof phone case, join in.
If staying through Songkran: waterproof phone case + dry bag for valuables, second-old-clothes outfit, sandals (no flip-flops — they fly off when wet). Most hotels have lobby-side water-free zones.
Respectful traditions: water on elders' hands as blessing (gentle); water on Buddha images as merit-making; family visits to temples. Avoid throwing water at monks, the elderly, or police.
Drive cautiously: 'seven dangerous days' (April 11-17) has highest road-fatality rate in Thailand. Police checkpoints intensified. Use Grab / taxi / hotel transport.
